claims.text: The AI Scientist generates novel research ideas, writes code, executes experiments, visualizes results, writes full scientific papers, and conducts simulated peer review using large language models.
claims.best_result: Papers exceeding acceptance threshold at a top machine learning conference as judged by automated reviewer.

claims.text: The AI Scientist-v2 eliminates reliance on human-authored code templates, generalizes across diverse machine learning domains, and employs a novel progressive agentic tree-search methodology managed by a dedicated experiment manager agent, with an enhanced AI reviewer component integrating a Vision-Language Model (VLM) feedback loop for iterative refinement of figure content and aesthetics.
claims.best_result: First fully AI-generated paper successfully navigating peer review with acceptance above human threshold

claims.text: Agent Laboratory is a framework that uses LLM agents to conduct research through three stages (literature review, experimentation, and report writing), producing code repositories and research reports with human feedback at each stage.
claims.best_result: 84% decrease in research expenses compared to previous autonomous research methods

claims.text: MLE-bench evaluates ML engineering capabilities by creating diverse tasks from Kaggle competitions, establishing human baselines using Kaggle leaderboards, and testing frontier language models with open-source agent scaffolds.

AIBuildAI-2 is a knowledge-enhanced agent with an external, evolving knowledge system that automatically builds AI models using a hierarchical knowledge structure that dynamically loads relevant context.
claims.best_result: First place on MLE-Bench with 70.7% medal rate

claims.text: Uses verbal feedback and memory to help agents improve across trials, motivating explicit failure memory and revision loops.

Demonstrates open-ended skill acquisition with memory and iterative exploration, relevant to long-running agent loops and durable lessons.
Introduces agent evaluation tasks and motivates structured benchmarks for agent behavior rather than isolated text quality.

SWE-agent introduces a custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that enables language model agents to autonomously use computers to solve software engineering tasks by enhancing their ability to create/edit code files, navigate repositories, and execute tests and programs.
